translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai in the tent of meeting, on the first of the second month, in the second year after their departure from the land of Egypt, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take the sum of the whole assembly of the children of Israel, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, every male, according to their polls;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from twenty years and upward, all that go forth to military service in Israel: ye shall number them according to their hosts, thou and Aaron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with you there shall be a man for every tribe, a man who is the head of his father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the men that shall stand with you: for Reuben, Elizur the son of Shedeur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Simeon, Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Judah, Nahshon the son of Amminadab;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Issachar, Nethaneel the son of Zuar;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Zebulun, Eliab the son of Helon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the children of Joseph: for Ephraim, Elishama the son of Ammihud; for Manasseh, Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Benjamin, Abidan the son of Gideoni;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Dan, Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Asher, Pagiel the son of Ocran;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Gad, Eliasaph the son of Deuel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Naphtali, Ahira the son of Enan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These were those summoned of the assembly, princes of the tribes of their fathers, the heads of the thousands of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron took these men who are expressed by their names,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and gathered the whole assembly together on the first of the second month. And they declared their pedigrees after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, according to their polls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah had commanded Moses, so he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Reuben, Israel's eldest son, their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, according to their polls, every male from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Reuben, were forty-six thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Simeon: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, those that were numbered of them, by the number of the names, according to their polls, every male from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Simeon, were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Gad: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Gad, were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Judah: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Judah, were seventy-four thousand six hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Issachar: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Issachar, were fifty-four thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Zebulun: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Zebulun, were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Joseph: of the children of Ephraim: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Ephraim, were forty thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Manasseh: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Manasseh, were thirty-two thousand two hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Benjamin: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Benjamin, were thirty-five thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Dan: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Dan, were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Asher: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Asher, were forty-one thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Naphtali: their generations, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, by the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: those that were numbered of them, of the tribe of Naphtali, were fifty-three thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are those that were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron numbered, and the princes of Israel, the twelve men: each one was for the house of his fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all those that were numbered of the children of Israel, according to their fathers' houses, from twenty years old and upward, all that went forth to military service in Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all they that were numbered were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Levites after the tribe of their fathers were not numbered among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah had spoken to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only thou shalt not number the tribe of Levi, neither take the sum of them among the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of testimony, and over all the vessels thereof, and over all things that belong to it: they shall bear the tabernacle, and all its vessels; and they shall serve it, and round about the tabernacle shall they encamp;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when the tabernacle setteth forward, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle encampeth, the Levites shall set it up; and the stranger that cometh near shall be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel shall encamp every man in his camp, and every man by his own standard, according to their hosts;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the Levites shall encamp round about the tabernacle of testimony, that there come not wrath upon the assembly of the children of Israel; and the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of testimony.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did so; according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses, so did they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel shall encamp every one by his standard, with the ensign of their father's house; round about the tent of meeting, afar off, opposite to it shall they encamp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [for] those encamping eastward toward the sun-rising [there shall be] the standard of the camp of Judah according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Judah shall be Nahshon the son of Amminadab;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, were seventy-four thousand six hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that encamp next unto him shall be the tribe of Issachar; and the prince of the sons of Issachar shall be Nethaneel the son of Zuar;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered thereof, fifty-four thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [With them shall be] the tribe of Zebulun; and the prince of the sons of Zebulun shall be Eliab the son of Helon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered thereof, fifty-seven thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that were numbered of the camp of Judah were a hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred, according to their hosts. They shall set forth first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The standard of the camp of Reuben shall be southward according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered thereof, forty-six thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that encamp by him shall be the tribe of Simeon; and the prince of the sons of Simeon shall be Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [with them shall be] the tribe of Gad; and the prince of the sons of Gad shall be Eliasaph the son of Reuel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that were numbered of the camp of Reuben were a hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty, according to their hosts. And they shall set forth second.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tent of meeting shall set forth, the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps; as they encamp, so shall they set forth, every man in his place, according to their standards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The standard of the camp of Ephraim according to their hosts shall be westward; and the prince of the sons of Ephraim shall be Elishama the son of Ammihud;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, forty thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by him shall be the tribe of Manasseh; and the prince of the sons of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, thirty-two thousand two hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [with them shall be] the tribe of Benjamin; and the prince of the sons of Benjamin shall be Abidan the son of Gideoni;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, thirty-five thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that were numbered of the camp of Ephraim were a hundred and eight thousand one hundred, according to their hosts. And they shall set forth third.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The standard of the camp of Dan shall be northward according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Dan shall be Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, sixty-two thousand seven hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that encamp by him shall be the tribe of Asher; and the prince of the sons of Asher shall be Pagiel the son of Ocran;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, forty-one thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [with them shall be] the tribe of Naphtali; and the prince of the sons of Naphtali shall be Ahira the son of Enan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his host, even those that were numbered of them, fifty-three thousand four hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that were numbered of the camp of Dan were a hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set forth last according to their standards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are those that were numbered of the children of Israel, according to their fathers' houses: all those that were numbered of the camps, according to their hosts, were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses: so they encamped according to their standards, and so they journeyed, every one according to their families, according to their fathers' houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the generations of Aaron and Moses in the day that Jehovah spoke with Moses on mount Sinai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, who were consecrated to exercise the priesthood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nadab and Abihu died before Jehovah when they offered strange fire before Jehovah in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no sons; and Eleazar and Ithamar exercised the priesthood in the presence of Aaron their father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bring the tribe of Levi near, and present them before Aaron the priest, that they may minister unto him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall keep his charge, and the charge of the whole assembly, before the tent of meeting, to do the service of the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall keep all the utensils of the tent of meeting, and the charge of the children of Israel, to do the service of the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons: they are wholly given to him out of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and his sons shalt thou appoint that they may attend to their priest's office; and the stranger that cometh near shall be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from among the children of Israel instead of every firstborn that breaketh open the womb among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for every firstborn is mine. On the day that I smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I hallowed unto me all the firstborn in Israel, both of man and beast; mine shall they be: I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Number the sons of Levi according to their fathers' houses, after their families; every male from a month old and upward shalt thou number them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses numbered them, according to the commandment of Jehovah, — as he had been commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Gershon according to their families: Libni and Shimei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Kohath according to their families: Amram and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Merari according to their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their fathers' houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Gershon, the family of the Libnites, and the family of the Shimites: these are the families of the Gershonites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, those that were numbered of them were seven thousand five hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The families of the Gershonites encamped behind the tabernacle westward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prince of the father's house of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the charge of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was: the tabernacle and the tent, its covering, and the curtain of the entrance to the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the hangings of the court, and the curtain of the entrance to the court, which surrounds the tabernacle and the altar, and the cords thereof for all its service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Kohath, the family of the Amramites, and the family of the Izharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites: these are the families of the Kohathites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, there were eight thousand six hundred, who kept the charge of the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The families of the sons of Kohath encamped on the side of the tabernacle southward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prince of the father's house of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their charge was the ark, and the table, and the candlestick, and the altars, and the utensils of the sanctuary with which they ministered, and the curtain, and all that belongs to its service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prince of princes of the Levites was Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest: he had the oversight of them that kept the charge of the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were six thousand two hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prince of the father's house of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. They encamped on the side of the tabernacle northward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the charge of the sons of Merari consisted in the oversight of the boards of the tabernacle, and its bars, and its pillars, and its bases, and all its furniture, and all that belongs to its service,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the pillars of the court round about, and their bases, and their pegs, and their cords.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those who encamped before the tabernacle eastward, before the tent of meeting toward the sunrising, were Moses, and Aaron and his sons, who kept the charge of the sanctuary for the charge of the children of Israel; and the stranger that cometh near shall be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered at the commandment of Jehovah, according to their families, all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty-two thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Number all the first-born males of the children of Israel from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the Levites for me (I am Jehovah) instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel; and the cattle of the Levites, instead of all the firstlings among the cattle of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses numbered, as Jehovah had commanded him, all the firstborn among the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the firstborn males, by the number of the names, from a month old and upward, according to their numbering, were twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites instead of their cattle; and the Levites shall be mine: I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for those that are to be ransomed, the two hundred and seventy-three of the firstborn of the children of Israel, which are in excess over the Levites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt take five shekels apiece by the poll, according to the shekel of the sanctuary shalt thou take them, — twenty gerahs the shekel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt give the money unto Aaron and unto his sons for those in excess among them who are to be ransomed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took the ransom-money from them that were over and above those who were ransomed by the Levites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the firstborn of the children of Israel he took the money, a thousand three hundred and sixty-five [shekels], according to the shekel of the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses gave the money of them that were ransomed to Aaron and to his sons, according to the commandment of Jehovah, — as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi, after their families, according to their fathers' houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old, all that enter into the service, to do the work in the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This shall be the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting: it is most holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the camp setteth forward, Aaron and his sons shall go in, and they shall take down the veil of separation and cover the ark of testimony with it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall put thereon a covering of badgers' skin, and shall spread over it a cloth wholly of blue, and shall put its staves [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And upon the table of shewbread they shall spread a cloth of blue; and put thereon the dishes, and the cups, and the bowls, and goblets of the drink-offering; and the continual bread shall be thereon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall spread upon them a cloth of scarlet, and cover it with a covering of badgers' skin, and shall put its staves [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall take a cloth of blue, and cover the candlestick of the light, and its lamps, and its snuffers, and its snuff-trays, and all the oil vessels thereof, wherewith they perform its service;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall put it and all the utensils thereof within a covering of badgers' skin, and shall put it upon a pole.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And upon the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue, and cover it with a covering of badgers' skin, and shall put its staves [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall take all the instruments of service, wherewith they serve in the sanctuary, and put them in a cloth of blue, and cover them with a covering of badgers' skin, and shall put them upon a pole.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall cleanse the altar of the ashes, and spread a purple cloth thereon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall put upon it all the utensils thereof, wherewith they perform service about it: the firepans, the forks, and the shovels, and the bowls, — all the utensils of the altar; and they shall spread upon it a covering of badgers' skin, and put its staves [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Aaron and his sons have ended covering the sanctuary, and all the utensils of the sanctuary, when the camp setteth forward, then afterwards the sons of Kohath shall come to carry it; but they shall not touch the holy things, lest they die. This is what the sons of Kohath have to carry in the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall have the oversight of the oil for the light, and the fragrant incense, and the continual oblation, and the anointing oil, — the oversight of the whole tabernacle, and of all that is therein, over the sanctuary, and over its furniture.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not cut off the families of the Kohathites from among the Levites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but this shall ye do unto them, that they may live, and not die, when they draw near unto the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in, and appoint them every one to his service and to his burden;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they shall not go in and see for a moment the holy things, lest they die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take also the sum of the sons of Gershon, according to their fathers' houses, after their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From thirty years old and upward to fifty years old shalt thou number them; every one that cometh to labour in the work, to perform the service in the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This shall be the service of the families of the Gershonites, in serving, and in carrying:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle, and the tent of meeting, its covering, and the covering of badgers' skin that is above upon it, and the curtain of the entrance to the tent of meeting,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the hangings of the court, and the curtain of the entrance, of the gate of the court, which surroundeth the tabernacle and the altar, and the cords thereof, and all the instruments of their service; and all that is to be done for these things shall they perform.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the commandment of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites, in all their carrying, and in all their service; and ye shall appoint unto them in charge all their carrying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting, and their charge shall be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Merari: after their families, according to their fathers' houses shalt thou number them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old shalt thou number them, every one that entereth into the labour, to perform the service of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this shall be the charge of their burden, according to all their service in the tent of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars thereof, and the pillars thereof, and bases thereof,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the pillars of the court round about, and their bases, and their pegs, and their cords, all their instruments, according to all their service; and by name ye shall number to them the materials which are their charge to carry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari, according to all their service in the tent of meeting, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron and the princes of the assembly numbered the sons of the Kohathites after their families, and according to their fathers' houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, every one that entered into the labour, for service in the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that were numbered of them according to their families were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are they that were numbered of the families of the Kohathites, every one that served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of Jehovah through Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that were numbered of the sons of Gershon, after their families, and according to their fathers' houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, every one that entered into the labour, for service in the tent of meeting,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even those that were numbered of them, after their families, according to their fathers' houses, were two thousand six hundred and thirty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon, all that served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, after their families, according to their fathers' houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, every one that entered into the labour, for service in the tent of meeting,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even those that were numbered of them according to their families, were three thousand two hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of Jehovah through Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All those that were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron and the princes of Israel numbered, after their families and according to their fathers' houses,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, every one that came to serve [in] the work of the service, and [in] the work of carrying, in the tent of meeting,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even those that were numbered of them, were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to the commandment of Jehovah they were numbered by Moses, every one for his service, and for his burden, and numbered by him, as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Command the children of Israel, that they put out of the camp every leper, and every one that hath an issue, and whosoever is defiled by a dead person:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: both male and female shall ye put out; outside the camp shall ye put them, that they defile not their camps, in the midst whereof I dwell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did so, and put them outside the camp: as Jehovah had said to Moses, so did the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, When a man or woman shall commit any of all the sins of man to work unfaithfulness against Jehovah, and that soul is guilty,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then they shall confess their sin which they have done; and he shall recompense his trespass according to the principal thereof, and shall add unto it the fifth part thereof, and give it unto him against whom he hath trespassed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the man have no kinsman to recompense the trespass unto, the trespass which is recompensed to Jehovah shall be the priest's, besides the ram of the atonement, wherewith an atonement is made for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every heave-offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel, which they present unto the priest, shall be his.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every man's hallowed things shall be his: whatever any man giveth the priest shall be his.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man's wife go astray, and commit unfaithfulness against him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a man lie with her carnally, and it be hid from the eyes of her husband, and she be defiled in secret, and there be no witness against her, and she have not been caught;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the spirit of jealousy come upon him, and he be jealous of his wife, and she have been defiled, — or if the spirit of jealousy come upon him, and he be jealous of his wife, and she have not been defiled,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then shall the man bring his wife unto the priest, and he shall bring her offering for her, a tenth part of an ephah of barley-meal; he shall pour no oil upon it, nor put frankincense thereon; for it is an oblation of jealousy, a memorial oblation, bringing iniquity to remembrance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall bring her near, and set her before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel; and the priest shall take of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle, and put it into the water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall set the woman before Jehovah, and uncover the woman's head, and put the memorial oblation in her hands, which is the jealousy offering; and in the hand of the priest shall be the bitter water that bringeth the curse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall adjure her, and say unto the woman, If no man have lain with thee, and if thou hast not gone astray in uncleanness, in being with another instead of thy husband, be free from this bitter water that bringeth the curse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thou hast gone astray to another instead of thy husband, and hast been defiled, and a man other than thy husband have lain with thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then the priest shall adjure the woman with the oath of cursing, and the priest shall say unto the woman: Jehovah make thee a curse and an oath among thy people, when Jehovah doth make thy thigh to shrink, and thy belly to swell;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and this water that bringeth the curse shall enter into thy bowels, to make the belly to swell, and the thigh to shrink. And the woman shall say, Amen, amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall write these curses in a book, and shall blot them out with the bitter water,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall cause the woman to drink the bitter water that bringeth the curse, that the water that bringeth the curse may enter into her for bitterness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall take out of the woman's hand the oblation of jealousy, and shall wave the oblation before Jehovah, and shall present it at the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall take a handful of the oblation as a memorial thereof, and burn it upon the altar; and afterwards he shall make the woman drink the water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he hath made her to drink the water, then it shall come to pass, if she have been defiled, and have committed unfaithfulness against her husband, that the water that bringeth the curse shall enter into her, for bitterness, and her belly shall swell, and her thigh shall shrink; and the woman shall become a curse among her people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if the woman have not been defiled, and be clean, then she shall be clear, and shall conceive seed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the law of jealousies, when a wife goeth astray to another instead of her husband and is defiled,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or when the spirit of jealousy cometh upon a man, and he is jealous as regards his wife; then shall he set the woman before Jehovah, and the priest shall do to her according to all this law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the man be free from iniquity, but that woman shall bear her iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If a man or a woman have vowed the special vow of a Nazarite, to consecrate themselves to Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink: he shall drink no vinegar of wine, nor vinegar of strong drink, neither shall he drink any liquor of grapes, nor eat grapes, fresh or dried.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the days of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine, from the seed-stones, even to the skin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head; until the days be fulfilled, that he hath consecrated himself to Jehovah, he shall be holy; he shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the days that he hath consecrated himself to Jehovah, he shall come near no dead body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall not make himself unclean for his father, or for his mother, for his brother, or for his sister when they die; for the consecration of hisGod is upon his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the days of his separation he is holy to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if any one die unexpectedly by him suddenly, and he hath defiled the head of his consecration, then he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing; on the seventh day shall he shave it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons, to the priest, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall offer one for a sin-offering, and the other for a burnt-offering, and make an atonement for him, for that he sinned by the dead person; and he shall hallow his head that same day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall [again] consecrate to Jehovah the days of his separation, and shall bring a yearling lamb for a trespass-offering. But the first days are forfeited, for his consecration hath been defiled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the law of the Nazarite on the day when the days of his consecration are fulfilled: he shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall present his offering to Jehovah, one yearling he-lamb without blemish for a burnt-offering, and one yearling ewe-lamb without blemish for a sin-offering, and one ram without blemish for a peace-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a basket with unleavened bread, cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and their oblation, and their drink-offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall present them before Jehovah, and shall offer his sin-offering and his burnt-offering:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall offer the ram, a sacrifice of peace-offering to Jehovah, with the basket of unleavened bread; the priest shall offer also his oblation and his drink-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Nazarite shall shave the head of his consecration at the entrance to the tent of meeting, and shall take the hair of the head of his consecration, and put it on the fire which is under the sacrifice of the peace-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them upon the hands of the Nazarite, after he hath shaven [the hair of] his consecration.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest shall wave them as wave-offering before Jehovah; it is holy for the priest, with the breast of the wave-offering and with the shoulder of the heave-offering; and afterwards the Nazarite may drink wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the law of the Nazarite who hath vowed: his offering to Jehovah for his consecration, beside what his hand is able to get; according to the vow which he vowed, so shall he do, according to the law of his consecration.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons, saying, On this wise ye shall bless the children of Israel: saying unto them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah bless thee, and keep thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah make his face shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah lift up his countenance upon thee, and give thee peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall put my name upon the children of Israel; and I will bless them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the day that Moses had completed the setting up of the tabernacle, and had anointed it, and hallowed it, and all the furniture thereof, and the altar and all its utensils, and had anointed them, and hallowed them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the princes of Israel, the heads of their fathers' houses, the princes of the tribes, they that were over them that had been numbered, offered;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they brought their offering before Jehovah, six covered waggons, and twelve oxen; a waggon for two princes, and an ox for each; and they presented them before the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take it of them, and they shall be for the performance of the service of the tent of meeting, and thou shalt give them unto the Levites, to each according to his service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took the waggons and the oxen, and gave them to the Levites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Two waggons and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershon, according to their service;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and four waggons and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their service, — under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none, for the service of the sanctuary was upon them: they bore [what they carried] upon the shoulder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes presented the dedication-gift of the altar on the day that it was anointed; and the princes presented their offering before the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, They shall present their offering for the dedication of the altar, each prince on his day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that presented his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl, of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one cup of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one young bullock, one ram, one yearling lamb, for a burnt-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one buck of the goats for a sin-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Nahshon the son of Amminadab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the second day offered Nethaneel the son of Zuar, prince of Issachar;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he presented his offering; one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour, mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one cup of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one young bullock, one ram, one yearling lamb, for a burnt-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one buck of the goats for a sin-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Nethaneel the son of Zuar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the third day, the prince of the children of Zebulun, Eliab the son of Helon:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: his offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the fourth day, the prince of the children of Reuben, Elizur the son of Shedeur.

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Elizur, the son of Shedeur.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the fifth day, the prince of the children of Simeon, Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one buck of the goats for a sin-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the sixth day, the prince of the children of Gad, Eliasaph the son of Deuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the seventh day, the prince of the children of Ephraim, Elishama the son of Ammihud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Elishama the son of Ammihud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the eighth day, the prince of the children of Manasseh, Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the ninth day, the prince of the children of Benjamin, Abidan the son of Gideoni.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the tenth day, the prince of the children of Dan, Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.

translations.name: Darby Bible

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the eleventh day, the prince of the children of Asher, Pagiel the son of Ocran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the twelfth day, the prince of the children of Naphtali, Ahira the son of Enan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His offering was one silver dish of the weight of a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for an oblation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one cup of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one young bullock, one ram, one yearling lamb, for a burnt-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one buck of the goats for a sin-offering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a sacrifice of peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five yearling lambs. This was the offering of Ahira the son of Enan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This was the dedication-gift of the altar, on the day when it was anointed, from the princes of Israel: twelve silver dishes, twelve silver bowls, twelve cups of gold:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: each silver dish of a hundred and thirty [shekels], and each bowl seventy: all the silver of the vessels was two thousand four hundred [shekels] according to the shekel of the sanctuary;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: twelve golden cups full of incense, each cup of ten [shekels], according to the shekel of the sanctuary: all the gold of the cups, a hundred and twenty [shekels].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the cattle for the burnt-offering was: twelve bullocks, twelve rams, twelve yearling lambs and their oblation; and twelve bucks of the goats for a sin-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the cattle for the sacrifice of the peace-offering was: twenty-four bullocks, sixty rams, sixty he-goats, sixty yearling lambs. This was the dedication-gift of the altar, after it had been anointed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with Him, then he heard the voice speaking to him from off the mercy-seat which was upon the ark of testimony, from between the two cherubim; and he spoke to Him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto Aaron, and say unto him, When thou lightest the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light over against the candlestick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron did so; he lighted the lamps thereof over against the candlestick, as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this was the work of the candlestick: [it was] of beaten gold; from its base to its flowers was it beaten work; according to the form which Jehovah had shewn Moses, so had he made the candlestick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take the Levites from among the children of Israel, and cleanse them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thus shalt thou do unto them, to cleanse them: sprinkle upon them water of purification from sin; and they shall pass the razor over all their flesh, and shall wash their garments, and make themselves clean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall take a young bullock and its oblation of fine flour mingled with oil; and another young bullock shalt thou take for a sin-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring the Levites before the tent of meeting; and thou shalt gather together the whole assembly of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring the Levites before Jehovah; and the children of Israel shall put their hands upon the Levites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron shall offer the Levites as a wave-offering before Jehovah from the children of Israel, and they shall perform the service of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bullocks, and thou shalt offer the one for a sin-offering, and the other for a burnt-offering, to Jehovah, to make atonement for the Levites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set the Levites before Aaron, and before his sons, and offer them as a wave-offering to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt separate the Levites from among the children of Israel, that the Levites may be mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards shall the Levites come in to do the service of the tent of meeting. And thou shalt cleanse them, and offer them as a wave-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they are wholly given unto me from among the children of Israel; instead of every one that breaketh open the womb, instead of every firstborn among the children of Israel, have I taken them unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all the firstborn among the children of Israel are mine, both of man and beast; on the day that I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I hallowed them to myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons, from among the children of Israel, to perform the service of the children of Israel in the tent of meeting, and to make atonement for the children of Israel; that there be no plague among the children of Israel, when the children of Israel draw near to the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron, and all the assembly of the children of Israel, did to the Levites according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses concerning the Levites: so did the children of Israel to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levites purified themselves from sin, and they washed their garments; and Aaron offered them as a wave-offering before Jehovah; and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards the Levites came in to perform their service in the tent of meeting before Aaron, and before his sons; as Jehovah had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so did they to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is that which concerneth the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward shall he come to labour in the work of the service of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from fifty years old he shall retire from the labour of the service, and shall serve no more;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he shall minister with his brethren in the tent of meeting, and keep the charge, but he shall not serve [in] the service. Thus shalt thou do unto the Levites with regard to their charges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after their departure from the land of Egypt, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the children of Israel also hold the passover at its set time;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: on the fourteenth day in this month between the two evenings, ye shall hold it at its set time; according to all the rites of it, and according to all the ordinances thereof shall ye hold it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke to the children of Israel, that they should hold the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they held the passover in the first [month] on the fourteenth day of the month, between the two evenings, in the wilderness of Sinai: according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were men, who were unclean through the dead body of a man, and could not hold the passover on that day; and they came before Moses and before Aaron on that day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those men said to him, We are unclean by reason of the dead body of a man: why are we kept back, that we may not present the offering of Jehovah at its set time among the children of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to them, Stay, and I will hear what Jehovah commands concerning you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If any one of you or of your generations be unclean by reason of a dead body or be on a journey afar off, yet he shall hold the passover to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the second month, on the fourteenth day, between the two evenings, shall they hold it; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs shall they eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall leave none of it until the morning, nor break a bone thereof: according to every ordinance of the passover shall they hold it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a man that is clean, and is not on a journey, and forbeareth to hold the passover, that soul shall be cut off from among his peoples; because he presented not the offering of Jehovah at its set time: that man shall bear his sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a stranger shall sojourn among you, and would hold the passover to Jehovah, according to the rite of the passover, and according to the ordinance thereof, so shall he do. Ye shall have one rite, both for the stranger and for him that is born in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the day that the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle of the tent of testimony; and at even it was upon the tabernacle as the appearance of fire, until the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So it was continually: the cloud covered it, and at night it was as the appearance of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the cloud rose from the tent, then the children of Israel journeyed; and at the place where the cloud stood still, there the children of Israel encamped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to the commandment of Jehovah the children of Israel journeyed, and according to the commandment of Jehovah they [remained] encamped; all the days that the cloud dwelt upon the tabernacle they encamped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the cloud was long upon the tabernacle many days, then the children of Israel kept the charge of Jehovah, and journeyed not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if it were so that the cloud was a few days upon the tabernacle, according to the commandment of Jehovah they encamped, and according to the commandment of Jehovah they journeyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if it were so that the cloud was there from the evening until the morning, and that the cloud was taken up in the morning, then they journeyed; or a day and a night, and the cloud was taken up, they journeyed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or two days, or a month, or many days, when the cloud was long upon the tabernacle, dwelling upon it, the children of Israel [remained] encamped, and journeyed not; but when it was taken up, they journeyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the commandment of Jehovah they encamped, and at the commandment of Jehovah they journeyed: they kept the charge of Jehovah according to the commandment of Jehovah through Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make thee two trumpets of silver; of beaten work shalt thou make them; and they shall serve for the calling together of the assembly, and for the journeying of the camps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they shall blow with them, the whole assembly shall gather to thee at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if they blow with one, then the princes, the heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when ye blow an alarm, the camps that lie eastward shall set forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when ye blow an alarm the second time, the camps that lie southward shall set forward; they shall blow an alarm on their setting forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the congregation is to be gathered together, ye shall blow, but ye shall not blow an alarm:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow with the trumpets; and they shall be to you for an everlasting statute throughout your generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarm with the trumpets; and ye shall be remembered before Jehovah yourGod, and ye shall be saved from your enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the day of your gladness, and in your set feasts, and in your new moons, ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt-offerings and over your sacrifices of peace-offering; and they shall be to you for a memorial before yourGod: I am Jehovah yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth of the month, that the cloud was taken up from off the tabernacle of the testimony.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel set forward according to their journeys out of the wilderness of Sinai; and the cloud stood still in the wilderness of Paran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they first took their journey, according to the commandment of Jehovah through Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The standard of the camp of the children of Judah set forward first according to their hosts, and over his host was Nahshon the son of Amminadab;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Issachar was Nethaneel the son of Zuar;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward bearing the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set forward according to their hosts, and over his host was Elizur the son of Shedeur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Kohathites set forward bearing the sanctuary: and [the others] set up the tabernacle whilst they came.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the standard of the camp of the children of Ephraim set forward according to their hosts, and over his host was Elishama the son of Ammihud;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the standard of the camp of the children of Dan set forward, the rear-guard of all the camps according to their hosts, and over his host was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ocran;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the host of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These were the settings forward of the children of Israel according to their hosts: so did they set forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Hobab, the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, We are journeying to the place of which Jehovah said, I will give it unto you: come with us, and we will do thee good; for Jehovah has spoken good concerning Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, I will not go; but to mine own land, and to my kindred will I go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Leave me not, I pray thee, because thou knowest where we are to encamp in the wilderness, and thou wilt be to us for eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, if thou come with us, that whatever good Jehovah doeth unto us, so will we do to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set forward from the mountain of Jehovah [and went] three days' journey; and the ark of the covenant of Jehovah went before them in the three days' journey, to search out a resting-place for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cloud of Jehovah was over them by day when they set forward out of the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the ark set forward, that Moses said, Rise up, Jehovah, and let thine enemies be scattered; And let them that hate thee flee before thy face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it rested, he said, Return, Jehovah, unto the myriads of the thousands of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that when the people murmured, it was evil in the ears of Jehovah; and Jehovah heard it, and his anger was kindled, and the fire of Jehovah burned among them, and consumed [some] in the extremity of the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people cried to Moses; and Moses prayed to Jehovah — and the fire abated.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they called the name of that place Taberah; because a fire of Jehovah burned among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the mixed multitude that was among them lusted; and the children of Israel also wept again and said, Who will give us flesh to eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt for nothing; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlic;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and now our soul is dried up: there is nothing at all but the manna before our eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the manna was as coriander seed, and its appearance as the appearance of bdellium.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The people went about, and gathered it, and ground it with hand-mills, or beat it in mortars, and boiled it in pots, and made cakes of it; and the taste of it was as the taste of oil-cakes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the dew fell upon the camp by night, the manna fell upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses heard the people weep throughout their families, every one at the entrance of his tent; and the anger of Jehovah was kindled greatly; it was also evil in the eyes of Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Jehovah, Why hast thou done evil to thy servant, and why have I not found favour in thine eyes, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have I conceived all this people, have I brought them forth, that thou sayest to me, Carry them in thy bosom, as the nursing-father beareth the suckling, unto the land which thou didst swear unto their fathers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whence should I have flesh to give unto all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give us flesh that we may eat!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am not able to bear all this people alone, for it is too heavy for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou deal thus with me, slay me, I pray thee, if I have found favour in thine eyes, that I may not behold my wretchedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and their officers; and take them to the tent of meeting, and they shall stand there with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will come down and talk with thee there; and I will take of the Spirit which is upon thee, and will put it upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, and thou shalt not bear it alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And unto the people shalt thou say, Hallow yourselves for to-morrow, and ye shall eat flesh; for ye have wept in the ears of Jehovah, saying, Who will give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt; and Jehovah will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not one day shall ye eat, nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, nor twenty days;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [but] for a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it become loathsome unto you; because that ye have despised Jehovah who is among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, The people in whose midst I am are six hundred thousand footmen; and thou sayest, I will give them flesh that they may eat a whole month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall flocks and herds be slaughtered for them, to suffice them? or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered for them, to suffice them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Hath Jehovah's hand become short? Now shalt thou see whether my word will come to pass unto thee or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went out and told the people the words of Jehovah; and he gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah came down in a cloud, and spoke to him, and took of the Spirit that was upon him, and put it upon the seventy men, the elders; and it came to pass, that when the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied, but they did not repeat [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And two men remained in the camp, the name of the one, Eldad, and the name of the other, Medad; and the Spirit rested upon them (and they were among them that were written, but they had not gone out to the tent); and they prophesied in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there ran a youth, and told Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua the son of Nun, the attendant of Moses, one of his young men, answered and said, My lord Moses, forbid them!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Moses said to him, Enviest thou for my sake? would that all Jehovah's people were prophets, [and] that Jehovah would put his Spirit upon them!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses withdrew into the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went forth a wind from Jehovah, and drove quails from the sea, and cast them about the camp, about a day's journey on this side, and about a day's journey on the other side, round about the camp, and about two cubits above the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people rose up all that day, and the whole night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: he that gathered little gathered ten homers; and they spread them abroad for themselves round about the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
